(00:28)
Who’s the audience? We put “They’ve tried countless diets and have lost and
regained the same 20 pounds or more time and time again. Often married,
weight has fluctuated a ton.” Basically, this is stuff that we just got from
the research and one of the big pain points or biggest fear in short-term.
Well, short-term, what are the big pain points here? We put like, “Losing and
regaining weight again and again.” What are their hopes and dreams right
now? Let’s go to the victories, the failures. “Struggling to feel normal their
whole lives,” I guess that should be longer term. “Cheating on their diets and
hating themselves,” getting that from this part want to have burgers and beer
without hating myself for it. “Beating themselves up.” Short-term; “feeling
hungry, cheating when out and about, stepping on the scale but no change
even after hard work.” I’m doing more than one to three of these out here then
we can highlight the most important ones. “Not losing weight fast enough,”
got that from over here. “Anger at themselves for not having willpower,”
similar to what I said before. “No support system. Not sexy or attractive to
spouse anymore. Also losing respect of spouse.” This one that’s about them
not having sex anymore.
(04:16)
Longer term, “stress in relationship, possible divorce, weight keeps creeping
up, feeling broken like they’re on a hamster wheel and can never get off.” I’ll
just put maybe health fears, too diabetes, heart disease. Although, they don’t
talk about that stuff as much.

(16:12)
Cell death. “For instance, Santosa and her colleagues look at the processes
of cell death and maintenance of healthy cells - apoptosis and autophagy,
respectively - that are usually associated with aging. Obesity-induced apoptosis
has been seen in mice hearts, livers, kidneys, neurons, inner ears and retinas.
Obesity also inhibits autophagy which can lead to cancer, cardiovascular
disease, type 2 diabetes and Alzheimer’s.”

levels of 27HC become excessively high, which can happen as a result of old
